qa_pairs.question: Why is it necessary to renew Alliance accounts annually?
qa_pairs.answer: The annual renewal of all user accounts is essential in order to collect up-to-date information required by our granting agencies and to deactivate accounts that are no longer in use.

qa_pairs.question: Who is required to renew an Alliance account?
qa_pairs.answer: All Alliance users must renew their account between May 6 and June 9, 2026. If you do not renew it, your account will be automatically deactivated after that date, meaning you will not be able to access your data, but it will not be deleted immediately. You can renew your account at any time after it has been deactivated.

qa_pairs.question: If I recently created my Alliance account, do I still need to renew it?
qa_pairs.answer: An exception is made for recent account holders: if you created your account after January 1, you do not have to renew it at this time. However, you still have to accept the new policies and consents, which will prompt on CCDB the first time you try to log in.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find the Privacy Policy and other agreements I have accepted?
qa_pairs.answer: All agreements that you are required to accept or indicate your preference for must be accepted on CCDB. Once signed, you can see them on the Agreements page. This includes Policies, Standards, Terms, and Procedures; Consent to Access User Data; Optional transfer of file ownership on account deactivation; and Consent to receive optional news and communications from the Digital Research Alliance of Canada. The policies are also available on the Alliance website.

qa_pairs.question: What is a primary role and what is its purpose?
qa_pairs.answer: Your primary role must correspond to your main position, generally the one you will hold for the longest. For example, a full-time faculty appointment should be your primary role over adjunct appointments, and for students or researchers, it should be the role with your primary supervisor. Primary roles are important for managing accounts with multiple roles, facilitating communication, and assisting with reporting. If you do not renew your primary role during the annual renewal period, your account will be deactivated.

qa_pairs.question: Can I change my primary role?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes. Users with multiple active roles can change their primary role at any time on CCDB's 'Home' page. Simply go to the role of your choosing and click on the 'Make this role primary' checkbox located next to it.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if my situation has changed (e.g., moving to a new faculty position or moving from Master's to PhD)?
qa_pairs.answer: If your role has changed, you must apply for a new role by logging into CCDB and going to 'My Account' → 'Apply for a new role'. If you need several roles, request one role at a time. Any new role approved is set as your primary role by default, but you can uncheck 'Make this role primary?' if you do not wish for it to be. If your current role(s) are no longer needed, click on 'disable old roles' to deactivate them, leaving your new role as the primary one.

qa_pairs.question: My account has expired and is deactivated, but I still need access to my data to move it off the Alliance Federation clusters. What should I do?
qa_pairs.answer: Please send an email to support@tech.alliancecan.ca so that we can assist you with your request.

qa_pairs.question: Who can I contact if my account renewal question is not answered in this FAQ?
qa_pairs.answer: Please send an email to renewals@tech.alliancecan.ca so that we can assist you with your request.

qa_pairs.question: As a faculty member, what are the consequences if I do not renew my account by the deadline?
qa_pairs.answer: Any active account not renewed between May 6 and June 9, 2026, will expire and be automatically deactivated after that date, though accounts can be reactivated at any time. If you are a faculty member with sponsored users and your account expires, your account as well as the accounts of all those users you sponsor will be deactivated. Any jobs submitted by you or your sponsored users will be paused, and your research group will lose access to their files until your sponsor account is reactivated. If your account or those of your sponsored users are no longer needed, you can let them expire; otherwise, you must request renewal or apply for a new role if your situation or that of your sponsored user(s) has changed.

qa_pairs.question: What steps are required to renew my account?
qa_pairs.answer: You will receive an email during the account renewal period with a link to the account renewal form on the CCDB. You must update your contact information and Field of Research, fill out a short User Satisfaction Survey, and update the status of your sponsored roles (e.g., students, postdocs). University faculty sponsoring other users will also be asked to update the status of these accounts. Once all information is updated and confirmed, and sponsored users' status is updated, you must click the 'REQUEST ACCOUNT RENEWAL' button on the left side of the form to initiate the renewal request.

qa_pairs.question: If I am a Principal Investigator (PI) with a faculty role moving to a different institution, will users sponsored with my old role be automatically transferred to my new role?
qa_pairs.answer: No. Any user you want to sponsor with your new faculty role will have to apply for a new role sponsored by your new faculty position. If you have a RAC allocation, this means that users sponsored with your old role will lose access to the Resource Allocation Project (RAP) where your resources are allocated. Once your sponsored users get their new roles activated, you may have to add them to your RAP by going to 'My Projects' → 'Manage Project Membership'.

qa_pairs.question: If I indicated on my account renewal form that a sponsored user has moved on and is no longer under my sponsorship, can I still access their files?
qa_pairs.answer: Consent from that user may be required for you to access the files you want. If you need access to such files, it is important to make arrangements with your sponsored user as soon as possible to mitigate the risk of a former sponsored user becoming incommunicado. For more information, please consult the Access to files of users leaving a project page.
